seoTitle: Ord River vs Osborne Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Ord River and Osborne Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Ord River vs Osborne Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ord River and Osborne Park.
heading: Ord River vs Osborne Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ord River, Western Australia and Osborne Park, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ord River has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Osborne Park (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ord River or Osborne Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ord River has a flood risk score of 60/100, while Osborne Park scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ord River scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Osborne Park scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ord River or Osborne Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ord River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
